candidates.label: Hyperbolic Absurd Claims
candidates.description: Use of exaggerated, surreal assertions about personal events or reality for humor.

citations.text: Sometimes I get a funny feeling In my central processing unit that I might be a robot.
I just invented a new iphone app called "words with people you would murder if you thought you could get away with it".

candidates.scope_note: Pattern observed within this batch only.
candidates.boundary_note: Not all posts exhibit such exaggeration; many are straightforward.

candidates.label: 'I wish' Opening
candidates.description: Posts begin with the first-person declarative 'I wish' followed by a personal desire or complaint.

citations.text: I've just declared myself "President of my cubicle" so I can have today off. I wish I would have thought of that before I actually went to work.
I wish @unitedairlines would unite my plane with a pilot so I could get out of Sacramento.

candidates.boundary_note: Other posts do not use this phrase; pattern is limited.

candidates.label: Hashtag Labeling
candidates.description: Inclusion of one or more '#tag' terms inserted mid- or end-of-post to categorize the statement.

citations.text: I hope the new Republican congress will pass a law to SEVERELY punish people who burn microwave popcorn at work. #officestinks
Currently jamming to some Donnie Iris. Hellz yeah. #80sjamz
Thank you Seahawks, you made my day. #datjustme

candidates.boundary_note: Some posts lack tags; pattern not universal.
